warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015
rules.notes: Compliance with GB2760-2015 is mandatory for the use of food additives in China.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015

rules.limit: Verification required via the Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) Online Database
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The GSFA (Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth conditions for permitted food additives in all foods. The database is regularly updated and users are encouraged to consult it and its Preamble.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.limit: Verification required via the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and levels established by the Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF)
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) for contaminants to be legally permitted in food or feed commodities to protect human health. The CCCF establishes and endorses these levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Follow the Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: This is a specific code of practice (CXC 78-2017) for reducing mycotoxin contamination in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices

rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: The EU Additives Database provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites in specific foods, and also process hygiene criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية - بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة
rules.notes: This technical regulation for prepackaged food labels is expected to include requirements for allergen declarations. Specifics on the list of mandatory allergens and declaration format require consulting the full text of the regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية - بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة
rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies the general requirements for labels of prepackaged foodstuffs. Specific details on content, format, and mandatory information require consulting the full text of the regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية - فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية
rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for certain food products, which must be declared on the label. Specific products and durations require consulting the full text of the regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية - فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية
rules.notes: This standard provides guidelines for optional shelf life periods for food products. If an optional shelf life is declared, it must comply with the standard. Specifics require consulting the full text of the standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 Gulf Technical Regulation - Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: Specific labelling requirements for prepackaged food stuffs must be verified by consulting the full GSO 9:2022 standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 Gulf Technical Regulation - HALAL FOOD - Part 1 : General Requirements
rules.notes: Specific general requirements for Halal food must be verified by consulting the full GSO 2055-1:2015 standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 Gulf Technical Regulation - Expiration dates for food products -Part 1 : Mandatory expiration dates
rules.notes: Specific mandatory expiration date requirements for food products must be verified by consulting the full GSO 150-1:2013 standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 Gulf Standard - Expiration dates for food products - Part 2 : Voluntary expiration dates
rules.notes: Specific voluntary expiration date guidelines for food products must be verified by consulting the full GSO 150-2:2013 standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.foodCategory: Foodstuffs intended for human consumption, food ingredients
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 1016:2015 - Microbiological Criteria for Foodstuffs
rules.notes: Specific microbiological limits for foodstuffs and food ingredients must be verified by consulting the full GSO 1016:2015 standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.parameter: Permitted food additives and their maximum levels
rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Food Additives Regulations
rules.notes: Food additives in seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks must comply with the specific provisions and maximum levels laid down in the FSSAI Food Additives Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Food_Additives_Regulations

rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling & Display Regulations
rules.notes: All food products, including seasoning and snack products, must declare specified allergens as per FSSAI Labelling Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display

rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling & Display Regulations
rules.notes: All food products, including seasoning and snack products, must comply with general labelling and display requirements as per FSSAI regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display

rules.sourceTitle: SE Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)
rules.notes: Specific provisions regarding the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: SE Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 ... tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)

rules.sourceTitle: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan
rules.notes: Specific limits for food additives, contaminants, microbiology, salt/sodium, allergens, and labelling requirements for processed foods are determined based on the food category as defined in this regulation. Verification of the specific food category and its associated limits is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan

rules.sourceTitle: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Verification of permitted food additives and their limits is required under the Food Sanitation Act.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food Additives

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Compliance with the Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food is required. Verification of specific limits is needed.
rules.evidenceQuote: Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of regulations related to Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy (BSE) in food products is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy(BSE)

rules.sourceTitle: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Compliance with the 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act' is required, which includes standards for contaminants in food. Verification of specific limits is needed.
rules.evidenceQuote: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc.

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of specific limits and regulations related to food contaminated by radioactivity is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Handling of food contaminated by radioactivity

rules.parameter: Food Produced by Recombinant DNA Techniques
rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of regulations related to food produced by recombinant DNA techniques is required, which may include specific labelling requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food Produced by Recombinant DNA Techniques

rules.parameter: Nutrition Labeling, Health Claims, Special Dietary Uses
rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of requirements for nutrition labeling, food with health claims, and food for special dietary uses is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food with Health Claims, Food for Special Dietary Uses, and Nutrition Labeling

rules.sourceTitle: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Compliance with the 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act' is required, which includes microbiological standards for food. Verification of specific limits is needed.
rules.evidenceQuote: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc.

rules.limit: Food manufacturers must be aware of all additives used and obtain information from suppliers
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Food manufacturers must be aware of all the ingredients, including additives, that are used in the products that they manufacture.

rules.foodCategory: All food products using Class II chemical preservatives
rules.parameter: Calculation for Class II chemical preservatives
rules.limit: Use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives if two or more are used
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Examples of Class II chemical preservatives include sorbic acid and benzoic acid.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.limit: Technological justification and no health risks to consumers
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives are allowed when: there is a technological justification they do not present health risks to consumers

rules.limit: Verification required based on additive and food product type
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Refer to Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.

rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies to all food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Verification required using SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.limit: Do not import or sell food products containing unpermitted additives
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of an additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.limit: Food businesses must be aware of common contaminants and conduct laboratory tests as needed
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Suppliers may have test information, or samples can be sent to SFA-recognized laboratories.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food businesses must be aware of the contaminants that are commonly found in their food products and conduct laboratory tests as needed.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.limit: Verification required based on contaminant type
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to relevant SFA documents for microbiological standards, heavy metals, mycotoxins, marine biotoxins, veterinary drug residues, pesticide residues, and other incidental constituents.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.limit: Singapore's regulatory limits apply; Codex MRLs apply if no local limits specified
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Verification required using SFA documents.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pesticide residues Singapore's regulatory limits Codex MRLs (if there are no regulatory limits specified in the Food Regulations)

rules.limit: Do not import or sell food products containing contaminants exceeding regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: Meat and food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Prohibition under Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hexene) hexoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-n-hexane) dienoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-2, 4-hexadiene)

rules.parameter: Veterinary drug residues (other than prohibited compounds)
rules.limit: Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) established by SFA
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website and will be included in future amendments of the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.limit: All ingredients and additives must be declared on food labels
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Separate standards for ready-to-eat food and non-ready-to-eat food (with different effective dates for the latter).
rules.evidenceQuote: For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food Non-ready-to-eat food (till 3 Mar 2024) Non-ready-to-eat food (from 4 Mar 2024)

rules.limit: Not required to be tested for TPC
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific use restrictions and labeling requirements for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) produced by microbial fermentation must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Food allergen labeling is required for products containing Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, and sulphite (as residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more mg/kg of food) as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Food allergen labeling is recommended for products containing Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined or purified sunflower seed oil), and Kiwis and kiwi products as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: If sulphite as residue sulphur dioxide is 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food, mandatory allergen labeling is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits for pesticide residues in foods must be verified against the official standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products
rules.notes: Specific limits for pesticide residues in animal products must be verified against the official standard. Relevant for processed foods containing animal-derived ingredients.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific regulations for nutrition claims on prepackaged food products must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: A precautionary allergen labeling statement is suggested if there is a risk of allergen cross-contamination, such as 'The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○' or synonymous terms.
rules.evidenceQuote: the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Specific limits and conditions for use of food additives must be verified from relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: All ingredients used in food products must be verified against the list of permitted and non-permitted ingredients.
rules.evidenceQuote: ส่วนประกอบที่อนุญาต/ไม่อนุญาตให้ใช้ในอาหาร

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Food products must comply with standards for contaminants.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Verification is required to ensure the food product is not on the list of prohibited foods for production, import, or sale.
rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.parameter: Toxic Substances and Veterinary Drug Residues
rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Food products must comply with standards for toxic substances and veterinary drug residues.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Food products in hermetically sealed containers must comply with Ministry of Public Health Notification No. 469 B.E. 2569.
rules.evidenceQuote: ประกาศกระทรวงสาธารณสุข (ฉบับที่ 469) พ.ศ. 2569 ออกตามความในพระราชบัญญัติอาหาร พ.ศ. 2522 เรื่อง อาหารในภาชนะบรรจุที่ปิดสนิท

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Food products must comply with general food labeling and nutrition labeling requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.sourceTitle: Thai FDA Food Division Website
rules.notes: Food products must comply with standards for pathogenic microorganisms.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS)
rules.notes: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30).
rules.evidenceQuote: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30)

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings; Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS)
rules.notes: Food additives and color additives must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173, 73, 74) or evaluated by FEMA/JECFA (for flavorings).
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS)
rules.notes: GRAS substances must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS)
rules.notes: Substances listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food

rules.limit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies
rules.notes: The FASTER Act declared sesame as the 9th major food allergen, effective January 1, 2023.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is a Guidance Level for Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an Action Level for 'Foods' generally.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Contaminant levels include tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies
rules.notes: Not required by law; can be used to address unavoidable cross-contact only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes and taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact. Must be truthful and not misleading.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies
rules.notes: Must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine... must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies
rules.notes: All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies
rules.notes: A final rule defines “gluten-free” for food labeling. Compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the “gluten-free” claim were established on August 12, 2020.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling... On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods... bearing the “gluten-free” claim.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies
rules.notes: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.
rules.evidenceQuote: Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement. Example: “Contains wheat, milk, and soy.”

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies
rules.notes: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear in parentheses following the name of the ingredient.
rules.evidenceQuote: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient. Examples: “lecithin (soy),” “flour (wheat),” and “whey (milk)”

rules.parameter: Specific Allergen Declaration (Tree Nuts, Fish, Shellfish)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies
rules.notes: The type of tree nut, and the species of fish and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies
rules.notes: Must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
rules.evidenceQuote: Various sulfiting agents... must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

rules.sourceTitle: Hệ thống văn bản Chính phủ
rules.notes: Specific limits for food additives in seasoning, snack food, and processed foods in Vietnam require verification by consulting official legal documents issued by the Ministry of Health or Ministry of Agriculture and Food Industry, accessible through the government's legal document system.
rules.evidenceQuote: Lĩnh vực: Y tế - Sức khỏe; Cơ quan ban hành: Bộ Y tế; Bộ Nông nghiệp và Công nghiệp thực phẩm

rules.sourceTitle: Hệ thống văn bản Chính phủ
rules.notes: Specific requirements for allergen declaration/list on seasoning, snack food, and processed foods in Vietnam require verification by consulting official legal documents issued by the Ministry of Health or Ministry of Agriculture and Food Industry, accessible through the government's legal document system.
rules.evidenceQuote: Lĩnh vực: Y tế - Sức khỏe; Cơ quan ban hành: Bộ Y tế; Bộ Nông nghiệp và Công nghiệp thực phẩm

rules.sourceTitle: Hệ thống văn bản Chính phủ
rules.notes: Specific limits for chemical contaminants in seasoning, snack food, and processed foods in Vietnam require verification by consulting official legal documents issued by the Ministry of Health or Ministry of Agriculture and Food Industry, accessible through the government's legal document system.
rules.evidenceQuote: Lĩnh vực: Y tế - Sức khỏe; Cơ quan ban hành: Bộ Y tế; Bộ Nông nghiệp và Công nghiệp thực phẩm

rules.sourceTitle: Hệ thống văn bản Chính phủ
rules.notes: Specific requirements for labelling warnings on seasoning, snack food, and processed foods in Vietnam require verification by consulting official legal documents issued by the Ministry of Health or Ministry of Agriculture and Food Industry, accessible through the government's legal document system.

rules.sourceTitle: Hệ thống văn bản Chính phủ
rules.notes: Specific microbiological limits for seasoning, snack food, and processed foods in Vietnam require verification by consulting official legal documents issued by the Ministry of Health or Ministry of Agriculture and Food Industry, accessible through the government's legal document system.
